title: SUTRO HEIGHTS PARK - San Francisco - Yelp
content: I absolutely LOVE our hikes here, life doesn't get better than this! Think tranquility, stunning oceanfront views, and calories burned. This was my 'hood'

title: Sutro Heights Park - A Great SF View!
content: # Sutro Heights Park. Sutro Heights Park is a delightful surprise sitting high above the Cliff House on the edge of San Francisco. The views of Ocean Beach and the coastline are spectacular, but not many people know it's there. Sutro Heights Park was the location of an elaborate estate and gardens built in 1881 by Adolph Sutro, an immigrant from Prussia who bought and developed much of the land in this part of San Francisco. In 1885 the gardens were opened to the public and the house remained occupied by the Sutro family until 1938, when Sutro's daughter Emma died and the land was donated to the city. Gate to sutro's estate above the Cliff HouseOriginal entrance to the Sutro Heights Park. The amazing views of the beach and ocean, as well as the western edge of San Francisco, are the best part of the Sutro Heights Park, IMO. There is also a view of the entrance to San Francisco Bay, and of course Seal Rock and the Cliff House below.

title: Sutro Heights Park Photos - Yelp
content: Amazingly beautiful view looking down toward ocean beach. beautiful park to sit and enjoy little sun and a good short hike.

title: Sutro Heights Park in San Francisco is a mystical, romantic delight
content: Person shot near Pride events in heart of S.F. # This mystical San Francisco park was once a posh getaway. Two people walk along a path with panoramic views of Golden Gate Park, the Great Highway and Ocean Beach atop Sutro Heights Park in San Francisco. A person sits on a bench at Sutro Heights Park in San Francisco, which overlooks the city’s western edge. 1886: The main drive running through Sutro Heights Park, where Adolph Sutro kept a home. A view of the Great Highway and Ocean Beach as seen from atop Sutro Heights Park. It was 1885 in San Francisco, and much of the city was still an unrealized dream. Sutro Heights Park was once the most posh corner of the city. Once the home of mayor Adolph Sutro, the grounds of Sutro Heights Park has always been open to the public and is now part of the Golden Gate National Recreation Area.
